steps.title: Identify the defining feature of the basking shark data
steps.content: The basking shark maintains body temperature 1.0–1.5 °C above ambient (showing heat retention), yet its temperature “declines as the ambient temperature falls.” Despite generating metabolic heat, its body temperature still varies with ambient conditions.

steps.title: Match this pattern to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice B concludes that both the Pacific halibut and basking shark have body temperatures “at least partly determined by the ambient water temperature.” The Pacific halibut, being ectothermic, is obviously ambient-dependent; the basking shark data shows it is too, despite its heat retention. Choices A, C, and D either claim basking shark's temperature is fixed (contradicting the data) or lack textual support.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
